home_meta_description: A gentle focus timer with a duration you choose and voluntary five-minute extensions.
about_meta_description: Why 5 Minute Grove makes starting small and choosing another five minutes the whole point.
about_heading: A tiny contract to help you begin.
about_paragraph_1: The hardest part of working, for me, is starting.
about_paragraph_2: Traditional focus timers never worked very well. A Pomodoro required me to commit to 25 minutes before I had even begun. A stopwatch had no visible end, and choosing a countdown meant deciding how long I ought to work. Each option made starting feel bigger than it needed to be.
about_paragraph_3: Five minutes felt different. It was small enough to be an honest commitment: I could focus for five minutes, and when they were over, I was allowed to stop.
about_paragraph_4: Often, though, starting was all I needed. When the timer ended, I found myself choosing another five minutes—and then sometimes another. Each block became a gentle checkpoint: continue if I wanted to, or finish without having failed.
about_paragraph_5: 5 Minute Grove is my attempt to wrap that way of working in a simple, cozy package. Start with five minutes. When the timer ends, add five more, switch tasks, or finish there. As you work, each focused minute helps your grove grow.
about_paragraph_6: It isn’t about squeezing as much work as possible out of every focus. It’s about making the first step approachable and letting momentum grow voluntarily.
notifications_unsupported: Notifications are not available in this browser.
notifications_denied: Notifications remain off because permission was not granted.
wellbeing_description: A gentle note after midnight, shown at most once a day.
grove_reset_description: Start the grove over without removing your focused time.
reset_grove_confirmation: Reset your grove? This removes all trees and leaves. Your focused time will stay, and this cannot be undone.
intro_paragraph_1: Use it like any timer, or use its real trick: choose a time you can honestly agree to—even less than five minutes.
intro_paragraph_2: When time is up, you’re free to stop. If momentum has arrived, add five minutes and keep your grove growing. Each extension is a choice, never an obligation.
sleep_reminder_body: The grove will be here tomorrow. Remember to leave a little time for rest, too.
focus_list_empty: Nothing planned. Add one small thing, or begin directly from the timer.
focus_list_open_tasks_reorder: Open tasks. Drag a task by its handle to change its position.
journal_empty: No notes yet. Time you choose to save will appear here.
timer_remove_minute_title: Remove one minute from this timer
timer_add_minute_title: Add one minute to this timer
timer_save_without_completing: Save this focus without completing the task
timer_save_and_complete: Save this focus and mark the task done
timer_end_body: Continue if you’d like, or finish here.
duration_editable_label: Timer duration in minutes and seconds
task_placeholder: What would you like to begin?
grove_tree_summary_one: {matureTrees} mature tree. Tree {treeNumber} has {leaves} of {leafLimit} leaves.
grove_tree_summary_other: {matureTrees} mature trees. Tree {treeNumber} has {leaves} of {leafLimit} leaves.
toast_contract_finished_away: Your five-minute contract finished while you were away.
toast_focus_assigned: This focus is now assigned to “{title}”.
toast_task_reopened: Task moved back to your list.
notification_body: {intention} has reached {blockLabel}. Add five minutes to continue.
